home.hero-tagline: 325 years of history. A legacy that transcends borders.
home.hero-subtitle: From the heart of Mesopotamia, a family's living heritage — preserved through centuries, shared with the world.
home.intro-text: The Hısrım family carries a legacy of more than three centuries. From Mardin, in the ancient lands of Mesopotamia, our roots extend across 15 countries — yet they remain inseparably tied to the soil, culture, and traditions of our origin.
home.wine-teaser-title: Born from 5,000 Years of Mesopotamian Soil
home.wine-teaser-text: From ancient vines rooted in volcanic earth, a wine of noble character emerges — aged in French oak, limited in production, infinite in legacy.

history.intro-text: The Hısrım family story is one of resilience, tradition, and deep connection to their land. For over three centuries, our family has preserved its identity, culture, and values — through times of prosperity and hardship alike.
history.origin-text: Our story begins in Mardin, in the heart of ancient Mesopotamia. A land where civilizations were born, where vine cultivation dates back 5,000 years. It is here that the Hısrım family laid its foundations, cultivating not only grapes but a way of life deeply intertwined with the land.
history.diaspora-text: Over the centuries, members of the Hısrım family have spread across the globe — to Europe, the Americas, the Middle East, and beyond. Yet despite the distance, the bond with Mardin and with each other has never wavered. The family legacy is a thread that connects generations across borders.
history.timeline-1700-text: The earliest records of the Hısrım family in the limestone lands of ancient Mesopotamia.
history.timeline-1850-text: The family deepens its roots in Syriac tradition. Winemaking becomes a sacred element of community life.
history.timeline-1900-text: Despite expansion across more than 15 countries, the connection to Mardin remains intact, passed down through generations.
history.timeline-now-text: A new chapter begins. The family legacy is revived through heritage preservation, cultural projects, and the continuation of ancestral traditions.
history.era1-text: The earliest records of the Hısrım family emerge from the limestone lands of ancient Mesopotamia. In the shadow of Mardin's cliffs, a family takes root — cultivating vines, weaving traditions, and building a legacy that would endure for centuries.
history.era1-detail: The name Hısrım first appears in the records of the Syriac Orthodox community of Mardin, linked to viticulture and sacred craft.
history.era2-text: The family deepens its roots in Syriac tradition. Winemaking becomes a sacred element of community life. The Hısrım artisans are known for their textiles — creating ceremonial vestments for the churches and monasteries of Tur Abdin.
history.era2-detail: Historical textiles woven by the family still survive in the collections of the Deyrülzafaran Monastery.
history.era3-text: A period of profound upheaval reshapes the region. Despite the turmoil, the Hısrım family endures — preserving its identity, protecting its heritage, and planting the seeds of a diaspora that would span the globe.
history.era3-detail: The family's bonds prove stronger than any border. Traditions, recipes, and stories are carried across continents.
history.era4-text: The Hısrım family branches extend across the world — Turkey, Syria, Brazil, Argentina, Spain, Germany, Australia, and beyond. Yet despite the distances, the connection to Mardin remains the heartbeat of the family.
history.era4-detail: 14 family branches documented across 15 countries: Helvacı, Hanaşe, Anache, Güzeliş, Özkardeşler, Gezer.
history.era5-text: A new chapter begins. The family legacy is revived through heritage preservation, cultural projects, and the continuation of ancestral traditions. The Hısrım wine returns to the land of its birth — each bottle carrying the weight of 325 years.
history.era5-detail: This website is itself part of the renewal — a living archive that grows with each family member's contribution.

genealogy.intro-text: The Hısrım genealogy spans more than three centuries and extends across 15 countries. This tree represents the known branches of our family — a living document that continues to grow as new connections are discovered.
genealogy.tree-note: This section is being expanded with verified family records. New members and branches will be added as documentation is gathered.

heritage.intro-text: The Hısrım family heritage encompasses historical artifacts, textiles, documents, and cultural treasures passed down through generations. This digital archive preserves and shares these invaluable pieces with the world.
heritage.textile-text: Among the most remarkable pieces in the family collection are historical textiles of exceptional craftsmanship. These works represent centuries of artistic tradition and cultural identity.
heritage.placeholder-text: Details and documentation for this piece are being prepared.
heritage.coming-soon: Additional pieces will be added to this archive as they are documented and photographed.

wines.intro-text: Hısrım wine is not a commercial product — it is a continuation of family tradition. Born from ancient vines in the volcanic soils of Mesopotamia, each bottle carries the weight of centuries of knowledge, care, and devotion.
wines.wine-text: Crafted from meticulously selected Mardin grapes, aged in French oak barrels specially imported for this purpose. A wine of noble character, long life, and uncompromising quality.
wines.legacy-text: This is more than winemaking — it is a cultural responsibility. Part of the proceeds from Hısrım wine support the Deyrülzafaran Monastery, preserving Syriac Orthodox traditions and the artisanal heritage of the region.
wines.terroir-text: The volcanic soils and white limestone of Mardin create extraordinary conditions for viticulture. Red earth rich in minerals, a climate sculpted over millennia, and ancient vines whose roots reach deep into history — together they produce grapes of unparalleled character. The wine born from this terroir can age gracefully for up to 20 years.
wines.mahzen-text: Deep in the cellars of Mardin, time works its quiet alchemy. Our Kırmızı Sek — Dry Red Wine — rests for 10 months in oak barrels specially imported from France. Here, in the darkness and silence, the wine develops the complex aromas and velvety character that define Hısrım.
wines.mahzen-detail: Limited production. Each bottle is a testament to patience, tradition, and the pursuit of the highest quality.

textile.intro-text: The Hısrım family possesses a remarkable collection of historical textiles — works of exceptional artistry that embody centuries of cultural identity and craftsmanship. This section is being developed to showcase these treasures.
textile.section-text: The textile heritage of the Hısrım family represents a chapter of extraordinary importance. These pieces are being carefully documented, restored, and prepared for presentation in this digital archive.
textile.placeholder-text: Detailed documentation of textile pieces, their historical context, techniques, and significance will be available here as the archive grows.
textile.cta-text: If you have information, photographs, or documentation related to Hısrım family textiles, please contact us to contribute to this archive.

mardin.intro-text: Mardin is more than a city — it is the cradle of our family's identity. Perched on a hillside overlooking the vast Mesopotamian plain, this ancient city has been home to civilizations for millennia and remains the spiritual heart of the Hısrım legacy.
mardin.land-text: The volcanic soils and limestone foundations of this region have nurtured vineyards for thousands of years. The unique microclimate of southeastern Anatolia creates conditions found nowhere else — producing grapes of unparalleled character.
mardin.monastery-text: The Deyrülzafaran Monastery, one of the oldest active monasteries in the world, stands as a testament to the region's spiritual and cultural depth. The Hısrım family maintains a deep connection to this sacred tradition.
mardin.identity-text: Mardin stands at the intersection of civilizations — Syriac, Kurdish, Arab, and Turkish traditions have coexisted here for centuries. This cultural richness is woven into the very fabric of the Hısrım family identity.

contact.intro-text: This website is a living archive — a work in progress that grows with each new document, photograph, and story contributed by family members around the world.
contact.contribute-text: If you are a member of the Hısrım family or possess documents, photographs, or objects related to our heritage, we invite you to share them. Every contribution helps preserve our collective memory for future generations.

footer.desc: A family heritage spanning 325 years. From Mardin to the world.
